summary: The deferral of production deployment/rollback/long-term ops is correctly and explicitly flagged for MVP posture (CONSTRAINTS "Deployment Constraints" production-hardening line; ASSUMPTIONS row "Production-grade deployment/rollback..."; RISKS §14 "MVP Security Simplifications"), and the local-first-plus-replay reliability stance is locked and coherent (DECISIONS ADR-009; REQUIREMENTS REQ-O-005). The 10-minute budget and lower-cap live override are locked with the override values legitimately left open pending provider research (CONSTRAINTS "Runtime Budgets"; OQ-013). However, the demo SAFETY-NET MECHANICS themselves — the thing the whole fallback strategy rests on — are underspecified in three load-bearing ways: (1) there is NO Postgres migration tooling decision anywhere despite an iterative 2-week schema-churning build on an append-only log; (2) schemaVersion is defined on the event envelope but has zero handling/upcast/compatibility spec, so a "prepared replay" captured under an older schema may not rebuild under newer projection code — directly endangering the safety net; (3) the prepared-replay capture→store→seed pipeline (how a good run becomes a committed fixture and gets loaded into the demo DB) is referenced everywhere but never specified. Local-vs-hosted parity is asserted as a goal but has no concrete enforcement mechanism. None of these contradict locked decisions; they are gaps that should be closed before task generation since the replay path is the demo's primary risk mitigation.

findings.finding: No Postgres migration/schema-management tooling is chosen anywhere (no Drizzle/Prisma/Kysely/raw-SQL-migration decision). For a 2-week iterative build with 10+ projection tables plus an append-only run_events log whose payload starts as JSONB and gets promoted into projections 'once stable' (DATA_MODEL line 36), schema will churn constantly. Without a migration mechanism, multiple engineers across four ownership surfaces cannot keep local DBs in sync, and any committed replay fixture can desync from the live schema, breaking the demo safety net.
findings.evidence: ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §13 (config files list omits migrations) and §16 (Deployment, Postgres 'running locally or containerized' with no schema management); DATA_MODEL.md line 36 (payload JSONB promoted into projections later); DECISIONS.md ADR-002/ADR-009 (Postgres locked, no tooling named)
findings.proposed_fix: Add a locked decision naming a lightweight migration tool (recommend Drizzle Kit or node-pg-migrate for TS-native, low-ceremony migrations) and a rule that every projection-table change ships a migration. State that local and hosted both run the same migration chain at boot, and that the seed/replay loader runs after migrations.

findings.finding: schemaVersion is a required field on every RunEventEnvelope but no doc specifies how it is handled. There is no upcaster/forward-compat rule, no statement of which component is responsible for reconciling old-schema events, and no compatibility guarantee. Because the prepared-replay fixture is captured once and replayed weeks later against evolving projection-builder code, an event captured at schemaVersion=N that the projection builder no longer understands will silently break replay — the exact failure mode the safety net exists to prevent (RISK-006 'event log missing data needed for replay').
findings.evidence: DATA_MODEL.md lines 30 (schemaVersion field) and 149-155 (Replay Rules say replay rebuilds from run_events ordered by sequence and must not call providers, but say nothing about schema version mismatch); ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §7 envelope; RISKS.md RISK-006
findings.proposed_fix: Add an explicit schemaVersion-handling rule: projection builders must accept all schemaVersions <= current via per-version upcasters (or a documented 'replay fixtures are re-captured whenever schemaVersion bumps' policy as the MVP-acceptable shortcut). State who owns upcasting (runtime/projection-builders package) and add a test (extend REQUIREMENTS REQ-T-003) that replays a fixture captured at an older schemaVersion.

findings.finding: The prepared-replay 'safety net' is cited as the central demo-failure mitigation in at least six places but its capture→store→seed pipeline is never specified: how a known-good run is exported from a live DB into a committed artifact, what format that artifact takes (SQL dump vs newline-JSON event stream vs fixture module), where it lives in the repo, and how the seed loader injects it into the demo Postgres before the showcase. Without this, 'replay data seeded' (§16) is an aspiration, not a build task.
findings.evidence: ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §16 ('Replay data seeded' bullet, no mechanism); §13 config lists 'demo problem sets' but no replay fixtures; REQUIREMENTS.md REQ-O-001/REQ-T-001/REQ-T-005; RISKS.md RISK-004 fallback 'prepared runs'; ASSUMPTIONS.md row 'A demo fallback path is required'
findings.proposed_fix: Specify a concrete pipeline: an export command (e.g. `GET /runs/:runId/replay` output or a `dump-replay` script) writes an ordered run_events JSON artifact under a versioned path (e.g. fixtures/replay/<runId>.json with its schemaVersion recorded); a `seed-demo` script loads it into the demo DB after migrations; the artifact is committed and regenerated on schemaVersion bumps. Add this as an explicit task surface (demo/observability owner).

findings.finding: Local-vs-hosted parity is named as a requirement ('hosted/local parity', 'two run modes to verify') but no parity-enforcement mechanism is specified — no shared bootstrap (same migrations + same seed loader + same env contract) and no statement that the replay fixture is identical across both. The risk register flags hosted deployment time cost (RISK-015) but not the subtler parity-drift risk where the local demo passes rehearsal and the hosted instance diverges.
findings.evidence: DECISIONS.md ADR-002 rationale line 180 ('hosted/local parity') and ADR-009 cons ('Two run modes to verify'); ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §16 (Local path and Hosted path described as separate bullet lists with no shared bootstrap); RISKS.md RISK-015
findings.proposed_fix: Add a one-line locked invariant: local and hosted run the identical boot sequence (migrate -> seed replay fixture -> start API/web) parameterized only by env/credentials, and the demo replay fixture is the same committed artifact in both. Make 'rehearse on whichever environment is the showcase target' an explicit rehearsal item alongside the existing low-cap/replay rehearsals (§14).

findings.finding: The lower-cap live override is locked as a concept but there is no statement that override caps must still respect the hard safety rails (energy/depth/spawn/time) as a floor-and-ceiling — i.e., the demo override may LOWER caps for speed but must never be a path to RAISE them past safe maxima. This is implied by the locked non-negotiable rails but not joined to the demo-override mechanism, leaving a small underspecification at the boundary.
findings.evidence: CONSTRAINTS.md 'Runtime Budgets' (demo-safe lower override) and 'Non-Negotiable Safety Rails'; RISKS.md RISK-003; trust-boundary 'Browser -> API ... cap max validation' (RISKS.md Trust Boundaries)
findings.proposed_fix: Add one sentence to §16/§12: the demo-override only reduces caps within validated maxima; the existing 'cap max validation' on the Browser->API boundary remains authoritative and rejects any override above the configured ceiling. No new mechanism needed; just tie the two together.

findings.finding: Whether thin deployment-level access control is required for the showcase is still open (OQ-011/OQ-014). This is the one deployment item that could pull real work into the timebox if the hosted instance is publicly exposed, yet it is unresolved with the showcase ~11 days out from the 2-week build.
findings.evidence: OPEN_QUESTIONS.md OQ-011 (status: open) and trailing open question on deployment-level access control; RISKS.md §14 'add thin deployment-level access control if hosted URL is public'; ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §16 ('Thin access control if publicly exposed')
findings.proposed_fix: Decision needed: is the showcase presented from a public hosted URL or a local/operator-driven machine? Recommended default: demo from local-first (per ADR-009), treat hosted as optional and, if pursued, gate it with a single reverse-proxy basic-auth credential rather than building any in-app auth — keeping product auth deferred as locked.
